title: The Tomb Slabs of Santa Croce: A New Sepltuario
highlights: This dissertation is a study of the tomb slabs of Santa Croce which were set in the pavement of the church before 1500. Of the one hundred and two examples, twenty-three are carved with effigies in low relief; the remaining are inlaid with colored marbles and bronze displaying familial coats-of-arms and inscriptions. The purpose of the thesis is to bring attention to a heretofore little known body of sculpture which remains largely in situ in the pavement of the church,  ...    and carve them. The body of the thesis is a catalogue of the tomb slabs arranged counter-clockwise in standard guide book order through the church. Each entry begins with the name and death date of the deceased, the location of the  ...    non-figural tomb slabs; (4) the history of Santa Croce; and (5) the sepoltuarii and other documentary sources. In "The Non-Figural Tomb Slab," the decorative motifs are traced from the mid-trecento to the end of the quattrocento. The works of Ghiberti and Donat

title: Tombs and memorials - Santa Croce
highlights: commemorated by a slab showing the deceased soldier in armour framed by a tabernacle in the Gothic style.  ...    The earliest example of a Renaissance tomb, the Tomb slab of Bartolomeo Valori (†1427) by Lorenzo Ghiberti, was to serve as a model for many others: portraits became more realistic with the elimination of the tabernacle, and allowance was made for the fact that they would be viewed from above. This form of burial lasted for centuries, as we can see from the Art Nouveau Tomb slab of Emilia Toscanelli Peruzzi (†1910).  ...    A new style of funerary monument that came into fashion in the 14th century consisted of a sarcophagus with additional sculptural elements. title: The History of Santa Croce - Santa Croce
highlights: After the transepts, construction began on the nave and aisles and the church was complete by the end of the 14th  ...    Over the centuries Santa Croce has become a "lieu de mémoire ", the Pantheon of an entire nation. Initially housing the graves of Franciscan friars, it soon became the burial place of choice for the neighbourhood's wealthier families, who were commemorated by gravestones on the floor in an attempt to conjugate a desire to indicate their place of burial with the wish to appear humble in keeping with the ideals of the mendicant orders.
